skill.name: firecrawl-build-scrape
skill.description: Integrate Firecrawl `/scrape` into product code for single-page extraction. Use when an app already has a URL and needs markdown, HTML, links, screenshots, metadata, or structured page output. Prefer this skill over broader crawl patterns when the feature is page-level.
skill.summary: Integrate Firecrawl scraping into product code for extracting content from single pages.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The detected patterns represent legitimate documentation formatting (markdown backticks, relative doc links) and intentional skill design (API key input for Firecrawl authentication). No security risks identified.

content.user_title: Extract Content from Any Web Page with Firecrawl
content.value_statement: Apps often have URLs but need clean page content. This skill provides a straightforward path to integrate Firecrawl scraping for single-page extraction into markdown, HTML, or structured data.
actual_capabilities: Extract page content as markdown or HTML for downstream processing
Retrieve links, metadata, and screenshots from single URLs
Filter to main content only for article-like pages
Support multiple output formats including JSON for structured extraction
Use wait options for JavaScript-rendered pages
Handle self-hosted Firecrawl deployments via custom API URL
limitations: Requires a known URL upfront; does not discover URLs
Single-page extraction only; use crawl for multi-page workflows
No interactive browser automation; escalate to interact skill for click/type flows
Rate limits and page complexity affect extraction quality

use_cases.title: Knowledge Base Ingestion
use_cases.description: Ingest content from documentation pages, blog posts, or articles into a vector database for semantic search.

use_cases.title: Market Research Automation
use_cases.description: Extract pricing, product details, or changelog information from competitor or industry websites.

use_cases.title: Content Quality Monitoring
use_cases.description: Monitor specific pages for content changes, extract metadata, or validate page structure.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the firecrawl-build-scrape skill to extract the main content from {URL} as markdown.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - extract content from a single known URL

prompt_templates.title: Structured Data Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use firecrawl-build-scrape to get the page at {URL} and extract all heading and paragraph content as structured markdown.

prompt_templates.title: JavaScript-Rendered Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use firecrawl-build-scrape to extract content from {URL} using a wait of 5 seconds to handle JavaScript rendering.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Format Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use firecrawl-build-scrape to extract markdown content and links from {URL}, then format the results for a database schema.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - extract and transform for specific output

output_examples.output: Page content extracted as clean markdown with headings, code blocks, and links preserved. Navigation and footer removed by default.
Main article text extracted with title, author, publication date, and body content. Sidebar ads and related articles filtered out.

best_practices: Start with markdown format for LLM consumption unless specific formatting is required
Use onlyMainContent filter for article pages with navigation noise
Consult official language-specific docs before writing integration code
anti_patterns: Do not use scrape when you need to discover URLs; use the search skill instead
Do not skip the wait option on JavaScript-heavy pages without testing first
Do not request multiple formats when only one is needed; keep the integration narrow

faq.question: When should I use `/scrape` instead of `/crawl`?
faq.answer: Use scrape when you have a specific URL and need content from that single page. Use crawl when you need to discover and extract from multiple pages.

faq.question: What format should I request from the API?
faq.answer: Default to markdown for LLM or search pipeline consumption. Use HTML for preserving structure, or JSON for programmatic parsing.

faq.question: How do I handle pages that load content with JavaScript?
faq.answer: Add a wait option (5-10 seconds) to allow the page to fully render before extraction.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with a self-hosted Firecrawl instance?
faq.answer: Yes, set the optional FIRECRAWL_API_URL input to your self-hosted endpoint instead of the default hosted service.

faq.question: What if the page has authentication or paywalls?
faq.answer: Scrape cannot bypass authentication. For authenticated content, you need browser-based extraction with cookies or session tokens.

faq.question: How do I extract only the main content without navigation?
faq.answer: Use the onlyMainContent option to filter out navigation, headers, and footers, keeping only the primary page content.
